cards.title: Packages
cards.body: Staycation, family, romance, wellness, workation, event, dining, and seasonal packages.

cards.title: Add-ons
cards.body: Breakfast, late checkout, early check-in, transfers, parking, amenities, and upgrades.

cards.title: Dining and spa
cards.body: Restaurant offers, brunches, tasting menus, spa treatments, wellness packages, and day passes.

cards.title: Experiences
cards.body: Tours, local activities, classes, destination moments, and partner-ready experiences.

cards.title: Events and groups
cards.body: Wedding inquiries, meeting packages, retreat paths, room blocks, and group stays.

items.question: What is a hotel Booking Engine?
items.answer: A hotel Booking Engine is the direct transaction layer that lets guests book rooms, packages, add-ons, and other hotel offers through a hotel-controlled path.

items.question: Should a Booking Engine sell more than rooms?
items.answer: Yes. A modern Booking Engine should support packages, add-ons, dining, spa, experiences, events, and Partner-ready offers where those products are part of the hotel strategy.

items.question: Why do packages matter?
items.answer: Packages give guests a clearer reason to book direct and make social or Partner recommendations easier to convert.

items.question: Where does Wink fit?
items.answer: Wink runs the Booking Engine as the transaction layer under direct booking, social selling, partner demand, Traveliko, and platform integrations - all at 5.5% all-in with Stripe included.

fields.heading: Ready to make hotel demand bookable?
fields.body: Start with the route that matches your team, then move into a focused demo or sign-up flow.
